input: What is the notice period required for contract termination?
actual_output: Either party may terminate the agreement with 30 days written notice.
retrieval_context: Section 12.1 Termination for Convenience: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.

input: What are the payment terms in the contract?
actual_output: Payment is due within 30 days of invoice date.
retrieval_context: Section 5.2 Payment Terms: All invoices are due and payable within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month.

input: What is the limitation of liability clause?
actual_output: Neither party's liability shall exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim.
retrieval_context: Section 14.3 Limitation of Liability: In no event shall either party's aggregate liability exceed the total fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the claim.

input: What intellectual property rights does the customer receive?
actual_output: The customer receives a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the software during the subscription term.
retrieval_context: Section 3.1 License Grant: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, Vendor grants Customer a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to access and use the Software solely for Customer's internal business purposes during the Subscription Term.

input: What is the governing law for disputes?
actual_output: The agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.
retrieval_context: Section 18.1 Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

input: What is the confidentiality obligation?
actual_output: Each party must keep the other's confidential information secret for 5 years after disclosure.
retrieval_context: Section 9.1 Confidentiality: Each party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the other party's Confidential Information and not to disclose such information to any third party for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure.

input: What warranties does the vendor provide?
actual_output: The vendor warrants that the software will perform materially in accordance with the documentation.
retrieval_context: Section 11.1 Limited Warranty: Vendor warrants that during the Subscription Term, the Software will perform materially in accordance with the applicable Documentation. This warranty does not apply to free trials or beta features.

input: What is the auto-renewal clause?
actual_output: The subscription automatically renews for successive one-year terms unless either party provides 60 days notice of non-renewal.
retrieval_context: Section 2.2 Renewal: Unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current Subscription Term, this Agreement will automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms.

input: What data protection obligations apply?
actual_output: The vendor must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect customer data.
retrieval_context: Section 10.2 Data Security: Vendor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational security measures designed to protect Customer Data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction.

input: What is the dispute resolution process?
actual_output: Disputes must first be escalated to senior management, then submitted to binding arbitration if unresolved.
retrieval_context: Section 17.1 Dispute Resolution: The parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good faith negotiation between senior management. If unresolved within 30 days, disputes shall be submitted to binding arbitration under AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules.

input: What are the indemnification obligations?
actual_output: The vendor will indemnify the customer against third-party claims that the software infringes intellectual property rights.
retrieval_context: Section 13.1 Vendor Indemnification: Vendor shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Customer from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Software, as provided by Vendor, infringes any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret.

input: What is the force majeure clause?
actual_output: Neither party is liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, such as natural disasters or government actions.
retrieval_context: Section 16.1 Force Majeure: Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure is caused by circumstances beyond such party's reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, war, or government actions.

input: What are the acceptable use restrictions?
actual_output: The customer may not reverse engineer, sublicense, or use the software for illegal purposes.
retrieval_context: Section 3.3 Restrictions: Customer shall not: (a) reverse engineer or decompile the Software; (b) sublicense or transfer the Software to third parties; (c) use the Software for any illegal purpose; or (d) remove any proprietary notices.

input: What is the SLA uptime commitment?
actual_output: The vendor commits to 99.9% monthly uptime for the production environment.
retrieval_context: Exhibit A - Service Level Agreement: Vendor commits to a monthly uptime of 99.9% for the production environment, measured on a calendar month basis. Scheduled maintenance windows are excluded from uptime calculations.

input: What are the termination for cause provisions?
actual_output: Either party may terminate immediately for material breach if the breach is not cured within 30 days of written notice.
retrieval_context: Section 12.2 Termination for Cause: Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of the breach.

input: What happens to data upon contract termination?
actual_output: The vendor will provide a data export for 30 days after termination, then delete all customer data within 60 days.
retrieval_context: Section 12.4 Data Return and Deletion: Upon termination, Vendor will make Customer Data available for export for thirty (30) days. Thereafter, Vendor will delete all Customer Data within sixty (60) days, unless legally required to retain it.

input: What is the assignment clause?
actual_output: Neither party may assign the agreement without the other's written consent, except in connection with a merger or acquisition.
retrieval_context: Section 18.3 Assignment: Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except that either party may assign this Agreement without consent in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets.

input: What are the audit rights?
actual_output: The customer has the right to audit the vendor's compliance with data protection obligations once per year with 30 days notice.
retrieval_context: Section 10.5 Audit Rights: Customer may, upon thirty (30) days prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year, audit Vendor's compliance with its data protection obligations under this Agreement.

input: What is the entire agreement clause?
actual_output: The agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations and agreements.
retrieval_context: Section 18.7 Entire Agreement: This Agreement, including all exhibits and order forms, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, and agreements.

input: What are the notice requirements?
actual_output: Notices must be in writing and delivered by email with confirmation or overnight courier.
retrieval_context: Section 18.4 Notices: All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered by: (a) email with written confirmation of receipt; or (b) overnight courier service. Notices are effective upon confirmed receipt.

input: What is the warranty disclaimer?
actual_output: Except for the limited warranty, the software is provided 'as is' without any other warranties.
retrieval_context: Section 11.3 Disclaimer: EXCEPT FOR THE LIMITED WARRANTY IN SECTION 11.1, THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED 'AS IS' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

input: What are the customer's payment obligations for overages?
actual_output: Usage exceeding the contracted limits will be billed at the overage rate specified in the order form.
retrieval_context: Section 5.4 Overage Fees: If Customer's usage exceeds the limits specified in the applicable Order Form, Customer will be charged for such excess usage at the overage rates set forth in the Order Form.

input: What is the non-solicitation clause?
actual_output: During the term and for one year after, neither party may solicit the other's employees.
retrieval_context: Section 15.1 Non-Solicitation: During the Term and for one (1) year thereafter, neither party shall directly solicit for employment any employee of the other party who was involved in the performance of this Agreement.

input: What are the export control obligations?
actual_output: The customer must comply with all applicable export control laws and may not export the software to prohibited countries.
retrieval_context: Section 18.5 Export Controls: Customer agrees to comply with all applicable export control laws and regulations. Customer shall not export, re-export, or transfer the Software to any country, person, or entity subject to U.S. export restrictions.

input: What is the severability clause?
actual_output: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force.
retrieval_context: Section 18.8 Severability: If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

input: What are the benchmarking restrictions?
actual_output: The customer may not publish benchmark results without the vendor's prior written consent.
retrieval_context: Section 3.4 Benchmarking: Customer shall not publish or disclose the results of any benchmark tests of the Software without Vendor's prior written consent.

input: What is the price adjustment clause?
actual_output: The vendor may increase fees by up to 5% annually upon 60 days notice.
retrieval_context: Section 5.5 Price Adjustments: Vendor may increase fees for renewal terms by up to five percent (5%) upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to Customer.

input: What are the insurance requirements?
actual_output: The vendor must maintain commercial general liability insurance of at least $2 million per occurrence.
retrieval_context: Section 13.4 Insurance: Vendor shall maintain, at its own expense: (a) Commercial General Liability insurance with limits of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence; (b) Cyber Liability insurance with limits of at least $5,000,000.

input: What is the waiver clause?
actual_output: Failure to enforce any provision does not constitute a waiver of the right to enforce it in the future.
retrieval_context: Section 18.9 Waiver: No failure or delay by either party in exercising any right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of that right. Any waiver must be in writing and signed by an authorized representative.

input: What are the professional services terms?
actual_output: Professional services are provided on a time-and-materials basis at the rates specified in the statement of work.
retrieval_context: Section 6.1 Professional Services: Any professional services requested by Customer shall be provided pursuant to a mutually agreed Statement of Work. Unless otherwise specified, professional services are provided on a time-and-materials basis at Vendor's then-current rates.

input: What is the source code escrow provision?
actual_output: The vendor will deposit source code into escrow, which is released to the customer if the vendor becomes insolvent.
retrieval_context: Section 3.5 Source Code Escrow: Vendor shall deposit the Software source code with a mutually agreed escrow agent. The source code shall be released to Customer if Vendor becomes insolvent, ceases operations, or materially breaches its maintenance obligations.

input: What are the subcontractor restrictions?
actual_output: The vendor may use subcontractors but remains responsible for their compliance with the agreement.
retrieval_context: Section 7.2 Subcontractors: Vendor may engage subcontractors to perform services under this Agreement, provided that Vendor remains responsible for the performance of such subcontractors and their compliance with the terms of this Agreement.

input: What is the most favored nation clause?
actual_output: If the vendor offers lower prices to similarly situated customers, it must offer the same pricing to this customer.
retrieval_context: Section 5.6 Most Favored Nation: If Vendor offers pricing to any similarly situated customer that is lower than the pricing offered to Customer for substantially similar products and volumes, Vendor shall offer Customer the same pricing.

input: What are the training obligations?
actual_output: The vendor will provide up to 40 hours of training per year at no additional charge.
retrieval_context: Section 6.3 Training: Vendor shall provide Customer with up to forty (40) hours of training per calendar year at no additional charge. Additional training may be purchased at Vendor's then-current training rates.

input: What is the change of control provision?
actual_output: The customer may terminate the agreement within 90 days if the vendor undergoes a change of control to a competitor.
retrieval_context: Section 12.5 Change of Control: Customer may terminate this Agreement upon ninety (90) days written notice if Vendor undergoes a Change of Control resulting in Vendor being acquired by or merged with a direct competitor of Customer.

input: What are the documentation requirements?
actual_output: The vendor must provide and maintain current documentation for all software features.
retrieval_context: Section 4.1 Documentation: Vendor shall provide Customer with access to current Documentation for the Software. Vendor shall update the Documentation to reflect material changes to the Software within thirty (30) days of such changes.

input: What is the beta features clause?
actual_output: Beta features are provided as-is without warranty and may be discontinued at any time.
retrieval_context: Section 4.3 Beta Features: Vendor may make beta or preview features available to Customer. Such features are provided 'as is' without warranty, are not subject to the SLA, and may be discontinued at any time without notice.

input: What are the compliance obligations?
actual_output: Both parties must comply with all applicable laws and regulations in performing their obligations.
retrieval_context: Section 18.6 Compliance with Laws: Each party shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in connection with its performance under this Agreement, including data protection, privacy, and export control laws.

input: What is the injunctive relief clause?
actual_output: Either party may seek injunctive relief for breach of confidentiality or intellectual property provisions without posting bond.
retrieval_context: Section 17.3 Injunctive Relief: Notwithstanding the dispute resolution provisions, either party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction for breach of confidentiality or intellectual property obligations without the requirement to post bond.

input: What are the support response time commitments?
actual_output: Critical issues must be acknowledged within 1 hour and resolved within 4 hours.
retrieval_context: Exhibit A - Support SLA: Priority 1 (Critical) issues: initial response within 1 hour, resolution target within 4 hours. Priority 2 (High) issues: initial response within 4 hours, resolution target within 1 business day.

input: What is the data processing agreement requirement?
actual_output: The parties must execute a data processing agreement if the vendor processes personal data on behalf of the customer.
retrieval_context: Section 10.1 Data Processing: To the extent Vendor processes Personal Data on behalf of Customer, the parties shall execute a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) that complies with applicable data protection laws including GDPR.

input: What are the minimum purchase commitments?
actual_output: The customer commits to a minimum annual spend of $500,000 over the three-year term.
retrieval_context: Section 5.1 Minimum Commitment: Customer commits to a minimum annual spend of five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000) for each year of the three (3) year Initial Term.

input: What is the publicity clause?
actual_output: The vendor may reference the customer as a client in marketing materials with prior written approval.
retrieval_context: Section 15.2 Publicity: Vendor may reference Customer as a client in Vendor's marketing materials, website, and press releases, subject to Customer's prior written approval for each specific use.

input: What are the escrow release conditions?
actual_output: Source code escrow is released upon vendor insolvency, cessation of business, or material breach of maintenance obligations.
retrieval_context: Section 3.5 Escrow Release: The escrow agent shall release the source code to Customer upon: (a) Vendor's insolvency or bankruptcy; (b) Vendor's cessation of business operations; or (c) Vendor's material breach of its maintenance and support obligations.

input: What is the limitation on consequential damages?
actual_output: Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, including lost profits.
retrieval_context: Section 14.2 Exclusion of Consequential Damages: IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

input: What are the record retention requirements?
actual_output: The vendor must retain records related to the agreement for 7 years after termination.
retrieval_context: Section 10.6 Record Retention: Vendor shall maintain records related to its performance under this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years following the termination or expiration of this Agreement.

input: What is the counterparts clause?
actual_output: The agreement may be executed in counterparts, including electronic signatures, each of which is an original.
retrieval_context: Section 18.10 Counterparts: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original. Electronic signatures shall be deemed valid and binding.

input: What are the vendor's background check obligations?
actual_output: The vendor must conduct background checks on personnel who will have access to customer systems.
retrieval_context: Section 7.3 Personnel: Vendor shall conduct background checks on all personnel who will have access to Customer's systems or facilities, in accordance with applicable law and Vendor's standard screening procedures.

input: What is the technology escrow fee?
actual_output: The customer pays an annual escrow maintenance fee of $5,000.
retrieval_context: Section 3.5 Escrow Fees: Customer shall pay an annual escrow maintenance fee of five thousand dollars ($5,000) to the escrow agent. Vendor shall bear all initial deposit and update fees.

input: What are the disaster recovery commitments?
actual_output: The vendor commits to a recovery time objective of 4 hours and recovery point objective of 1 hour.
retrieval_context: Exhibit A - Business Continuity: Vendor commits to a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of four (4) hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of one (1) hour for the production environment.
